name: SaaS Startup — Series A Strategy
description: A B2B SaaS startup post-seed with $2M ARR, 50 customers, preparing Series A. The projector maps structural tensions to reveal where to focus, what risks are hidden, and which moves propagate.
direction: Scale from $2M to $8M ARR in 18 months while maintaining unit economics and team coherence

tensions.claim: Core product solves a real pain for mid-market companies but enterprise deals require features we don't have yet
Moving upmarket increases ACV but lengthens sales cycles and requires dedicated account management
Hiring fast to capture market window but each bad hire costs 6 months and poisons culture
Technical debt from MVP phase slows new feature development but rewriting now delays revenue growth
80% of leads come from one channel — high efficiency but existential risk if that channel degrades
Founders close 90% of deals — deep customer insight but zero scalability past 20 deals per quarter
Current burn gives 14 months runway — enough to hit milestones but no margin for error or pivot
Monthly churn at 3% is acceptable for now but compounds to 31% annual — unsustainable at scale
Customers want a platform but platforms take 3x longer to build and the tool already works
Main competitor just raised $30M — they'll outspend us on marketing but their product is weaker
Each customer generates data that improves the product for all — network effect but requires critical mass we don't have
European market is underserved and willing to pay but localization and compliance cost 2 engineers for 6 months
